    SELECT c.latitude, c.longitude
    FROM Student s
    JOIN City c ON s.city_code = c.city_code
    WHERE s.Fname = 'Linda' AND s.Lname = 'Smith'
),
StudentDistances AS (
    SELECT 
        s.Fname,
        s.Lname,
        -- Haversine formula to calculate distance in kilometers
        6371 * ACOS(
            COS(RADIANS(lc.latitude)) * COS(RADIANS(c.latitude)) *
            COS(RADIANS(c.longitude) - RADIANS(lc.longitude)) +
            SIN(RADIANS(lc.latitude)) * SIN(RADIANS(c.latitude))
        ) AS distance
    FROM Student s
    JOIN City c ON s.city_code = c.city_code
    CROSS JOIN LindaCity lc
    WHERE NOT (s.Fname = 'Linda' AND s.Lname = 'Smith')
)
SELECT Fname, Lname
FROM StudentDistances
WHERE distance = (SELECT MAX(distance) FROM StudentDistances);

    -- Anchor: find the starting collection
    SELECT Collection_ID, Parent_Collection_ID, Collection_Name
    FROM Collections
    WHERE Collection_Name = 'Nice'
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive step: go up the hierarchy
    SELECT c.Collection_ID, c.Parent_Collection_ID, c.Collection_Name
    FROM Collections c
    INNER JOIN Ancestors a ON c.Collection_ID = a.Parent_Collection_ID
)
SELECT Collection_Name
FROM Ancestors
WHERE Collection_Name != 'Nice'
ORDER BY Collection_ID;
